channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: La lezione del low-code che i CIO possono applicare all’agentic AI
item.description: Con il passare del tempo, la GenAI si sta trasformando sempre di più da un chatbot a cui si fanno domande, a un set di strumenti ai quali si chiede di fare qualcosa e l’agentic AI [in inglese] – l’espressione un po’ goffa con la quale si definisce questo approccio – sta rendendo sempre più chiaro ch
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO
item.description: Katie Arrington's return to DoD comes as her lawyers work out a settlement with the government related to the suspension of her security clearance in 2021. The post Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Crackdown on power-guzzling data centers may soon come online in California
item.description: A spate of new bills in California and other states would protect consumers from electricity rate hikes that subsidize data centers.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple introduces the iPhone 16e, a fast and powerful mid-range smartphone
item.description: Apple’s new iPhone 16e combines three innovations in one $599 package: an A18 chip, Apple’s fast smartphone processor; Apple Intelligence, the company’s private-by-design take on artificial intelligence; and the C1, Apple’s first-ever 5G radio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oracle extends support for on-prem Oracle Database 19c
item.description: As the status of an on-premises version of Oracle Database 23ai remains in limbo, the company has tossed a bone to its on-prem Oracle Database 19c customers in the form of an extension of Premier Support to Dec. 31, 2029, and Extended Support to Dec. 31, 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: iPhone 16e launch: Apple discontinues iPhone SE, iPhone 14, and iPhone 14 Plus
item.description: The announcement of the new iPhone 16e has also seen Apple quietly discontinue three iPhones, ending the iPhone SE product line in the process.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: iPhones may soon be able to use satellite connections indoors
item.description: Sending messages via satellite on iPhones should soon be as easy to as using your mobile network signal, as Apple and satellite partner Globalstar put a new satellite constellation into space.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Netflix is getting its first-ever English medical procedural series and a historical drama that looks like a Spanish Bridgerton
item.description: Netflix has announced its first English-language medical procedural series and a Spanish period drama that looks straight out of Bridgerton.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: HubSpot and Canva team up to level the creative playing field
item.description: New integration allows users to create on-brand content in minutes, including email templates, social posts, and landing pages.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Venture Capital kills ‘more value than it creates,’ Bay Area author says
item.description: In a new book, World Eaters, tech activist Catherine Bracy says Silicon Valley needs to focus more on solving real-world problems and less on fast profit.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Data Breach Exposes 2.7 Billion Records: What You Need to Know
item.description: A massive data breach has exposed nearly 2.7 billion records, highlighting the need for improved cybersecurity measures in the IoT industry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: I’ve got two fresh iPhone 17 rumors for you, but one of them stinks
item.description: New iPhone 17 rumors: Samsung displays tipped for all models, along with a crazy design change for the iPhone 17 Pro Max.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Aryaka adds AI-powered observability to SASE platform
item.description: SASE vendor Aryaka is looking to alleviate network alert fatigue with a little help from AI. Today Aryaka announced its Winter 2025 platform update, introducing its new AI Observe tool for advanced threat detection and prevention.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE’s firings and layoffs so far
item.description: Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple TV+ releases a gritty new crime drama trailer for Dope Thief that looks like a stylish version of The Wire
item.description: Apple TV+'s violent Philadelphia-set show has snagged the legendary Hollywood helmer and will start airing in March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A huge leak reveals the sequel to Sony's 5-star budget ANC earbuds, including specs and a cool new see-through look
item.description: Two years after Sony released some of the best budget earbuds on the market, the electronics manufacturer looks set to do it again.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Prince fans will finally get to hear Purple Rain in Dolby Atmos, as the classic movie comes to Dolby Cinemas for a one-night-only screening
item.description: The film Purple Rain will be re-released with better visuals and audio for a one-night-only event at Dolby Cinemas across the United States and the United Kingdom.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: 5 ways to fix OneDrive’s worst annoyances on Windows
item.description: On Windows, OneDrive is built right into the operating system. Even if you don’t turn it on, it will pop up occasionally, asking you to “back up your files to the cloud.” What that really means, of course, is that Microsoft wants you to move your folders to OneDrive. I’m not here to knock OneDrive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: More used Seagate HDDs disguised as new: Fraudsters make it tough to spot the difference
item.description: The controversy surrounding used Seagate hard drives has significantly widened, with fraudsters now targeting the company’s IronWolf and IronWolf Pro NAS drives alongside the previously affected Exos server-grade HDDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Más allá del ‘hype’: cuatro casos de uso de IA generativa que sí han funcionado
item.description: Si 2023 fue el año de la experimentación con la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa, 2024 fue cuando las empresas se centraron en casos de uso y comenzaron a poner en producción proyectos piloto.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A New App Brings Personal Security to the Masses - Uber-Style
item.description: A new app promises to make hiring personal security as simple as ordering an Uber, with on-demand bodyguards and private motorcades available in NYC and LA.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Bay Area job market sickened due to COVID has yet to recover
item.description: The Bay Area job market, an engine for California's economy, has sputtered badly in the wake of the coronavirus outbreak.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future
item.description: A recent challenge sponsored by NIST tasked drone designers with creating a system to help first responders map indoor environments. The post How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: 7 time-saving Linux commands
item.description: Some Linux commands can make your time on the command line easier and more productive. This post covers some commands that can come in very handy. 1.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mexico presses case against Google over Gulf of America name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum accused Google on Monday of violating the country's sovereignty, leaving open the possibility of a lawsuit over the tech company labeling the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America in its maps feature.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Mistral turns focus toward regional LLMs with Saba release
item.description: French AI startup Mistral is turning its focus toward providing large language models (LLMs) that understand regional languages and their parlance as a result of rising demand among its enterprise customers. “Making AI ubiquitous requires addressing every culture and language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to keep AI hallucinations out of your code
item.description: It turns out androids do dream, and their dreams are often strange. In the early days of generative AI, we got human hands with eight fingers and recipes for making pizza sauce from glue. Now, developers working with AI-assisted coding tools are also finding AI hallucinations in their code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3 key features of Postman’s AI Agent Builder
item.description: The software landscape is shifting from passive business processes to dynamic, AI-driven workflows. AI agents—systems that interact with APIs, make decisions, and execute complex tasks—are at the forefront of this transformation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: For February’s Patch Tuesday, Microsoft rolls out 63 updates
item.description: Microsoft released 63 patches for Windows, Microsoft Office, and developer platforms in this week’s Patch Tuesday update. The February release was a relatively light update, but it comes with significant testing requirements for networking and remote desktop environments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Buckle up for faster Python programs
item.description: In this edition of our biweekly Python newsletter: The next version of Python brings an all-new variety of speed boost. Python packages may soon pack better labeling for their ingredients. Cython 3.1 tees up new features for converting Python to C.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps
item.description: Creating a forward-thinking framework that keeps sight of what came before makes disruption and digital maturity possible, says Leidos CIO Alexandra Guenther. The post How to mature your IT infrastructure forward while also mining backward for gaps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
